TRANSCRIPT
Okay - Operation Quick Start and we are going to talk about tools now. So, first and
foremost, I just have to say, no matter what your tools are, in terms of building your
online business and there's a number that you need, there is no perfect solution. No
one size fits all for any of these solutions. For any of these tools. So, give up finding the
perfect tool. The perfect solution. And know that over the years you might have to change.
And also know that what I am using might not be right for you because my business has
grown. It’s quite sizable. I've got a team. I've got help. I've got lots of resources. So, I
might be using a tool that is not right for you.
Now, one of the awesome things is that tools are just, well, they are ten times easier
than they used to be, they are ten times better than they used to be, they are ten times
more powerful and they keep on getting better. But even so, they can still be frustrating at
times. The great news now compared to not too many years ago is that most all of them
have really good tutorials. Excellent tutorials. And also YouTube and Google are your
friend in terms of finding additional resources, or additional training, or additional
tutorials, no matter what tools we are talking about.
Now as I just mentioned, the stuff I use is probably not the stuff you should use if
you are just starting out. I'm going to go ahead and tell you what I use, but I do have a
team and lots of resources and I also probably have needs that you don't have when you
are just starting out. And remember that none of your decisions and tools are
permanent. All of them you can switch.
So, first one, that's near and dear to my heart, is your email service
provider. This is your email list host. This is where you keep your list of subscribers and
then when you want to send an email to them you actually will go into this tool, put your
email in, and that will send that to everyone. And that's how I can send out an email to
100,000 or 200,000 people all at once. Now, I personally real prize stableness and
continuity. I don't like bouncing around between email providers, and I want that one
that is just really strong and stable. I am willing to sacrifice on other things like fancy
features or ease of use, which isn't to say that the ones I have used are not easy to use, but
I'm just saying that the number one thing for me is stability and something that I can
stick with.
And deliverability is the absolute number one thing with email
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

TRANSCRIPT
Operation Quick Start Bonus Part 4 – Tools
3
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
providers. That means can they get your email into people's inbox and not have it
filtered out as spam. So, deliverability is absolutely paramount. That is the most
important thing. And it does, unfortunately, tend to be a moving target. Some host will
have great deliverability and then they'll go downhill. So, it's a moving target, but some
host are better than others.
Second factor is speed. By this I mean how fast can they get your emails out.
And this more applies once your list gets larger. When you first start out it's easy for a list
of 500 people or 100 people or 5,000 people or 10,000 people. It’s easy to get that email
out pretty quick. But if you have 50,000, or 70,000, or 100,000. Some host might get that
email out a lot slower. And so deliverability is number one speed is next.
Then after that, the features are the next most powerful. And be able to
segment your lists because you will want to end up treating different groups differently.
Like the number one most important is buyers versus non buyers. Once you start your
business you are going to have people join your list as prospects and then some of them
buying from you. And you have a fundamentally different relationship with those two
groups. And you need to be able to send different emails to the different groups. And then
also be able to track people by action and by interest. So if they are clicking through
emails on a certain topic then it's great to give those people more emails about that topic.
And so being able to segment your list or create multiple list is powerful and most all of
the email service providers offer this functionality in one way or another.
So, what I have used for many, many, many years is AWeber. And we didn't switch
from AWeber until just a year or two ago. And that was because we moved to Infusionsoft,
which just had a lot more capabilities that had the email deliverability built into the
shopping cart. And being able to integrate those two. Once we got advanced enough that
became something that we really, really wanted to do. So we currently use Infusionsoft.
And then so of our broadcast emails, our bigger broadcast emails, we use a service called
Maropost, and they integrate with Infusionsoft and they are good with getting out the
large broadcast. So we use Infusionsoft for automated follow-ups after maybe someone
purchases from us or after they first join our list. And then we use Maropost for our big
broadcast.
Now, there's lots and lots or other tools. Just because we are using these and if you
use Aweber, it doesn't mean that's the be all and end all, there's lots and lots of other
tools. Lots of people like MailChimp. You can get started there for free. I would strongly
urge you to read the terms of service. The last time I looked at MailChimp, which is a
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

while ago as I record this because reading terms of service is not like the most fun thing in
the world for me to do, but at one point they forbid you to do any affiliate marketing. So
you could not promote someone else's offer as an affiliate, they prohibited that. And so I
would not want to use an email service provider that dictates what you can sell and what
you can't sell. But a lot of people like MailChimp because it's easier to use and it's free for
the first, I don't know how many subscribers, but it's free to get started and it isn't until
you build up you list a little bit that they start charging you. Another one that a lot of
people like right now, is sort of hot, is ConvertKit. And there are lots of others. So, do
remember, just because I am not using them, doesn't make them bad. There are lots and
lots of different email host.
Now, the next thing you are going to need is some kind of a page
builder. Something that builds your opt-in pages, that builds your funnels, that builds
you sales pages. And this is the most awesome thing that has happened in the last few
years is the rise of some really good page builders. It used to be really hard to create a web
page. I mean really painful. And good design used to be hard to do and if you went and
got someone to do it - it would be really expensive. But now there are these great page
building tools and ones we have used, very happily, we used Leadpages and we used them
extensively at LeadPages.net. And then SamCart, is another great one, I am actually an
investor in that company because I really believe in it. And then ClickFunnels is another
one that is just growing like mad. So those are three really great page builders. Definitely
check them out. You don't have to build from scratch. It used to be such a pain in the neck
to build pages and these will do them for you. There are others but these are three that I
have experience with.
Okay. Other stuff…is like your shopping cart or your CRM, which is your Customer
Relationship Management tool. And here we use Infusionsoft and we have been using
them for, we are in our 3rd year now and its incredibly powerful. It has a whole ecosystem
of tools that plug into it that you can use. It is more complicated and its more expensive.
It’s probably not where I would start out. And then a competitor to Infusionsoft is
Ontraport and they're also a very powerful CRM. And then the one that I really like, I just
mentioned them for a page builder but it's also a great cart, is SamCart. And that's a lot
easier to use a lot less inexpensive to get started with. Again, I am an investor there, so I
am biased, but SamCart is a great tool to get started with.
And then in terms of creating a website, if you need to build pages outside of just
the pages that you could do with a page builder, and you want to build out a full website
for some reason or a blog, then WordPress is just the tool that almost everyone uses.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

There are so many extensions. There is so much that you can do with WordPress that it's
absolutely ridiculous and you would be crazy anything besides WordPress.
So then membership sites, lots of people want to use membership sites. We use
Memberium and that integrates really tightly with Infusionsoft. So, it’s a great set of tools.
Again, it’s fairly advanced. I have got a team putting it together for me. I am not doing it
myself. You could also use Kajabi is a lot more limited but it’s still really, really powerful.
And when I say limited, I mean you sort of, have to use their designs out of the box. You
can't customize it like you can with Memberium but it’s also a lot easier to use. And then
ClickFunnels. And then there is a bunch of different plugins for WordPress that you can
also use.
And then, in terms of creating a digital PDF - like for lead magnets... Boy, you can
create a PDF with just about anything. You know there is Adobe PDF Creator if you want
to go top end. Microsoft Word will create PDFs. Mac Pages will create PDFs. Even Google
Docs will create PDFs. So that is something that has changed in the last few years. It
wasn't always that easy.
In terms of video, for video hosting you can use YouTube, you can use Wistia, that's
one we use quite a bit, and Vimeo. Those are three winners. For creating video, I was not
a natural, I just got better at it over time. Remember it's not about the camera, it’s about
what you say and how you say it. You can literally use a phone and create great videos.
And I have published and I still publish videos that I shoot out of my phone. And it works
just great.
The next step up is a camcorder and the next step up from their is a DSLR. Then
you can get as fancy as you want. I personally, in my business, we have gotten pretty
fancy. We are using pro level DSLRs. It has been a progression for us. And like I said, I
still will release videos that I shoot on my phone. If you want to go deep on video, we have
a program that was created by my son Dan, who does all of our video. He does all my
video and he is absolutely amazing. He created a program called Easy Video
Awesomeness (EVA). And as I record this, this is not available at the minute but by the
time you listen to this it might be - Version 2.0. So send an email to
support@ProductionLaunchFormula.com and ask them about EVA which is Easy Video
Awesomeness.
Going forward from video editing, there are lots of great tools. I use Adobe
Premiere which is actually a pro level tool but I find that easier to use than a lot of the

other beginner tools. And I do very little video editing. Dan does 99 percent of it but then
now and then I will put out a simple video that I do myself and I use Adobe Premiere.
Sony Vegas Movie Studio is a great intro level video editor for Widows. iMovie is included
on all the MACs, so that beginner level. Camtasia is available for Widows and the MAC,
that's a great program. And ScreenFlow is available for the MAC. Those are all video
creation, video editing programs. And then if you want to create physical products, like
CD's, DVDs, books, anything like that, use Disk.com. And I have been using Disk.com
since 2005.
It’s been a long time. So, those are some of the tools. One of the things is these
tools keep evolving, new tools keep on coming up. This portion of the course, I
guarantee you, parts of it will become outdated quickly. But what you need to remember
is that you just have to get started. You just have to pick a tool, pick a few tools, and just
get started. Many of these - there could be three or four different choices and you could sit
there and spin your wheels trying to figure out what the perfect solution is and just
remember there is no perfect solution.
And then in terms of getting the stuff working, pretty much all the vendors have
great tutorials and these days their support is getting pretty darn good. And if you are still
stuck, check out YouTube or Google and see if there are other tutorials that are available.
Again, remember one thing, don't get into that paralysis analysis or analysis paralysis.
Whichever it is. When you just sit there and try to make the perfect decision because
there is no perfect decision. The perfect decision is just to pick one and get
started.
